title: trochodontales
intro: The rank Trochodontales is an order of flowering plants, typically comprising families that share distinct morphological characteristics. Examples of families within the Trochodontales order include Orchidaceae, Euphorbiaceae, and Cyperaceae. These families often display unique features such as specialized pollination mechanisms, diverse leaf structures, and adapted growth habits. However, it's worth noting that the Trochodontales order might not be universally recognized or classified in all botanical systems.

families.family_desc: Trochodendraceae is a family of flowering plants native to East Asia, commonly known as the warty-stemmed family due to the presence of warty growths on the stems of some members.
